# Enum Tools

% start short_desc

**Tools to expand Python's enum module.**

% end short_desc

% start shields

```
.. list-table::
        :stub-columns: 1
        :widths: 10 90

        * - Docs
          - |docs| |docs_check|
        * - Tests
          - |actions_linux| |actions_windows| |actions_macos| |coveralls|
        * - PyPI
          - |pypi-version| |supported-versions| |supported-implementations| |wheel|
        * - Anaconda
          - |conda-version| |conda-platform|
        * - Activity
          - |commits-latest| |commits-since| |maintained| |pypi-downloads|
        * - QA
          - |codefactor| |actions_flake8| |actions_mypy|
        * - Other
          - |license| |language| |requires|
```

% end shields

This library provides the following:

1. `enum_tools.autoenum` – A [Sphinx](https://www.sphinx-doc.org) extension to document Enums better than `autoclass`
   can currently.
2. `@enum_tools.documentation.document_enum` – A decorator to add docstrings to `Enum` members
   from a comment at the end of the line.
3. `enum_tools.custom_enums` – Additional `Enum` classes with different functionality.

## Installation

% start installation

`enum_tools` can be installed from PyPI or Anaconda.

To install with `pip`:

```bash
$ python -m pip install enum_tools
```

To install with `conda`:

```bash
$ conda install -c conda-forge enum_tools
```

% end installation

## Further Reading

1. <https://docs.python.org/3/library/enum.html>
2. [Is it possible to define a class constant inside an Enum?](https://stackoverflow.com/q/17911188/3092681)
3. [Enums with Attributes](https://stackoverflow.com/a/19300424/3092681)
4. [When should I subclass EnumMeta instead of Enum?](https://stackoverflow.com/a/43730306/3092681)

documentation: Custom subclasses of {class}`enum.Enum` and {class}`enum.Flag`.

custom_enums.py

functions.name: enum_tools.custom_enums._decompose
documentation: Extract all members from the value.

classes.name: enum_tools.custom_enums.MemberDirEnum
documentation:
{class}`~enum.Enum` which includes attributes as well as methods.

This will be part of the {mod}`enum` module starting with Python 3.10.

:::{seealso}
Pull request {pull}`19219 <python/cpython>` by Angelin BOOZ, which added this to CPython.
:::

:::{versionadded} 0.6.0
:::

classes.name: enum_tools.custom_enums.IntEnum
documentation: {class}`~enum.Enum` where members are also (and must be) ints.

classes.name: enum_tools.custom_enums.StrEnum
documentation: {class}`~enum.Enum` where members are also (and must be) strings.
def __int__(self):
return self.value
def __eq__(self, other):
if int(self) == other:
return True
else:
return super().__eq__(other)

classes.name: enum_tools.custom_enums.AutoNumberEnum
documentation: {class}`~enum.Enum` that automatically assigns increasing values to members.

classes.name: enum_tools.custom_enums.OrderedEnum
documentation: {class}`~enum.Enum` that adds ordering based on the values of its members.

classes.name: enum_tools.custom_enums.DuplicateFreeEnum
documentation: {class}`~enum.Enum` that disallows duplicated member names.

classes.name: enum_tools.custom_enums.IterableFlag

{class}`~enum.Flag` with support for iterating over members and member combinations.

This functionality was added to Python 3.10's {mod}`enum` module in {pull}`22221 <python/cpython>`.

:::{versionadded} 0.5.0
:::

This functionality was added to Python 3.10's {mod}`enum` module in {pull}`22221 <python/cpython>`.

functions.name: enum_tools.documentation.document_enum
documentation:
Document all members of an enum by parsing a docstring from the Python source..

The docstring can be added in several ways:

1. A comment at the end the line, starting with `doc:`:

   ```python
   Running = 1  # doc: The system is running.
   ```

2. A comment on the previous line, starting with `#:`. This is the format used by Sphinx.

   ```python
   #: The system is running.
   Running = 1
   ```

3. A string on the line *after* the attribute. This can be used for multiline docstrings.

   ```python
   Running = 1
   """
   The system is running.

   Hello World
   """
   ```

If more than one docstring format is found for an enum member
a {exc}`MultipleDocstringsWarning` is emitted.

```
param an_enum

An  subclass

type an_enum



returns

The same object passed as an_enum. This allows this function to be used as a decorator.

rtype

```

:::{versionchanged} 0.8.0  Added support for other docstring formats and multiline docstrings.
:::

functions.name: enum_tools.documentation.document_member
documentation:
Document a member of an enum by adding a comment to the end of the line that starts with `doc:`.

```
param enum_member

A member of an  subclass
```

classes.name: enum_tools.documentation.DocumentedEnum
documentation:
An enum where docstrings are automatically added to members from comments starting with `doc:`.

:::{note}
This class does not (yet) support the other docstring formats {deco}`~.document_enum` does.
:::
